question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 sets `passive enable` on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R3↔R2 link,
Area 0) under router ospf 100; R2 keeps its side active. A passive
OSPF interface stops sending Hellos and forms no adjacency: R3 tears
the R2 adjacency down immediately at config time (reason "interface
down or detached"), and R2 — no longer receiving Hellos — declares the
neighbor down about one dead interval (~40 s) later (reason "dead
timer expired"). The interface itself stays up. Unlike the
area-mismatch fault on the same link there are NO %ROUTING-OSPF-4-ERRRCV
packet errors and no BGP session flaps (loopback reachability reroutes
via R3's other area-0 link).
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.103 (R3), interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R3<->R2 link, area 0) was set to OSPF passive (`passive enable` under router ospf 100), while R2 keeps that link active.
A passive OSPF interface sends no Hellos and forms no adjacency: R3 drops the adjacency to R2 immediately, and R2, no longer hearing Hellos from R3, declares the neighbor down when its dead timer expires. The R2<->R3 adjacency over GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 cannot re-form and no routes are exchanged across that link; the interface itself remains up and there is no area or authentication mismatch.
Fix: remove the passive override on R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (`no passive` under router ospf 100 area 0) so Hellos resume and the adjacency reforms.
expected_symptoms_summary:
OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "interface down or detached" — immediate, at config-commit time (the passive end drops first).
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired" — about one dead interval later (the peer times out).

Absent by design (discriminators vs the area-mismatch fault on the same link):
- No OSPF packet receive errors (%ROUTING-OSPF-4-ERRRCV mismatch area ID) on either end.
- No BGP session state changes (loopback reachability reroutes via R3's other area-0 link).

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 passive enable

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 no passive

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:Jul 13 15:21:47.188 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: interface down or detached, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:Jul 13 15:22:20.043 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
